ksa_text: Knowledge of AI and machine learning-specific model risk considerations, including data drift, concept drift, black-box explainability requirements, model instability, and how traditional model risk frameworks must adapt for non-parametric, ensemble, deep learning, and foundation models.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ensure that AI design and development activities are properly documented and updated.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

onet.top_element_label: Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Although AI Governance is an edge domain for the Director of Analytics, this knowledge KSA presents sufficient signal (composite score 0.549) and conceptual alignment to include at the director level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (AI Governance), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.549). External reviewers may question whether this specific knowledge KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of Analytics versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act (Article 9), and OECD AI Principles confirm AI governance as a critical competency for responsible AI leadership.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with AI Governance appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Director of Analytics.

decision_rationale: Edge-domain AI Governance knowledge KSA cleared adversarial review with score 0.549. Included based on sufficient framework alignment and role relevance for the Director of Analytics.
